author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the words of the Gospel, John vi. 9, where the miracle of the five loaves and the two fishes is related.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, John vi. 9, where the miracle of the five loaves and the two fishes is related.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It was a great miracle that was wrought, dearly beloved, for five thousand men to be filled with five loaves and two fishes, and the remnants of the fragments to fill twelve baskets.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A great miracle: but we shall not wonder much at what was done, if we give heed to Him That did it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He multiplied the five loaves in the hands of them that brake them, who multiplieth the seeds that grow in the earth, so as that a few grains are sown, and whole barns are filled.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But, because he doth this every year, no one marvels.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Not the inconsiderableness of what is done, but its constancy takes away admiration of it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But when the Lord did these things, He spake to them that had understanding, not by words only, but even by the miracles themselves.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The five loaves signified the five books of Moses’ Law.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The old Law is barley compared to the Gospel wheat.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In those books are great mysteries concerning Christ contained.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whence He saith Himself, “If ye had believed Moses, ye would believe Me also; for he wrote of Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But as in barley the marrow is hid under the chaff, so in the veil of the mysteries of the Law is Christ hidden.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As those mysteries of the Law are developed and unfolded; so too those loaves increased when they were broken.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And in this that I have explained to you, I have broken bread unto you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The five thousand men signify the people ordered under the five books of the Law.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The twelve baskets are the twelve Apostles, who themselves too were filled with the fragments of the Law.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The two fishes are either the two precepts of the love of God and our neighbour, or the two people of the circumcision and uncircumcision, or those two sacred personages of the king and the priest.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As these things are explained, they are broken; when they are understood, they are eaten.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us turn to Him who did these things.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He is Himself “The Bread which came down from heaven;” but Bread which refresheth the failing, and doth not fail; Bread which can be tasted, cannot be wasted.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This Bread did the manna also figure.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wherefore it is said, “He gave them the Bread of heaven, man ate Angels’ Bread.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who is the Bread of heaven, but Christ?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But in order that man might eat Angels’ Bread, the Lord of Angels was made Man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if He had not been made Man, we should not have His Flesh; if we had not His Flesh, we should not eat the Bread of the Altar.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us hasten to the inheritance, seeing we have hereby received a great earnest of it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: My brethren, let us long for the life of Christ, seeing we hold as an earnest the Death of Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How shall He not give us His good things, who hath suffered our evil things?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In this our earth, in this evil world, what abounds, but to be born, to labour, and to die?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Examine thoroughly man’s estate, convict me if I lie: consider all men whether they are in this world for any other end than to be born, to labour, and to die?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is the merchandize of our country: these things here abound.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To such merchandize did that Merchantman descend.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And forasmuch as every merchant gives and receives; gives what he has, and receives what he has not; when he procures anything, he gives money, and receives what he buys: so Christ too in this His traffic gave and received.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what received He?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That which aboundeth here, to be born, to labour, and to die.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what did He give?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To be born again, to rise again, and to reign for ever.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: O Good Merchant, buy us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why should I say buy us, when we ought to give Thee thanks that Thou hast bought us?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou dost deal out our Price to us, we drink Thy Blood; so dost thou deal out to us our Price.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And we read the Gospel, our title deed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We are Thy servants, we are Thy creatures: Thou hast made us, Thou hast redeemed us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Any one can buy his servant, create him he cannot; but the Lord hath both created and redeemed His servants; created them, that they might be; redeemed them, that they might not be captives ever.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For we fell into the hands of the prince of this world, who seduced Adam, and made him his servant, and began to possess us as his slaves.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But the Redeemer came, and the seducer was overcome.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what did our Redeemer to him who held us captive?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For our ransom he held out His Cross as a trap; he placed in It as a bait His Blood.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He indeed had power to shed His Blood, he did not attain to drink it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And in that he shed the Blood of Him who was no debtor, he was commanded to render up the debtors; he shed the Blood of the Innocent, he was commanded to withdraw from the guilty.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He verily shed His Blood to this end, that He might wipe out our sins.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That then whereby he held us fast was effaced by the Redeemer’s Blood.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For he only held us fast by the bonds of our own sins.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They were the captive’s chains.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He came, He bound the strong one with the bonds of His Passion; He entered into his house into the hearts, that is, of those where he did dwell, and took away his vessels.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We are his vessels.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He had filled them with his own bitterness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This bitterness too he pledged to our Redeemer in the gall.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He had filled us then as his vessels; but our Lord spoiling his vessels, and making them His Own, poured out the bitterness, filled them with sweetness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us then love Him, for He is sweet.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Taste and see that the Lord is sweet.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He is to be feared, but to be loved still more.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He is Man and God; the One Christ is Man and God; as one man is soul and body: but God and Man are not two Persons.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In Christ indeed there are two substances, God and Man; but one Person, that the Trinity may remain, and that there be not a quaternity introduced by the addition of the human nature.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How then can it be that God should not have mercy upon us, for whose sake God was made Man?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Much is that which He hath done already; more wonderful is that which He hath done, than what He hath promised; and by that which He hath done, ought we to believe what He hath promised.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For that which He hath done, we should scarcely believe, unless we also saw it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Where do we see it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In the peoples that believe, in the multitude that has been brought unto Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For that hath been fulfilled which was promised to Abraham; and from these things which we see, we believe what we do not see.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Abraham was one single man, and to him was it said, “In thy seed shall all nations be blessed.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If he had looked to himself, when would he have believed?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He was one single man, and was now old; and he had a barren wife, and one who was so far advanced in age, that she could not conceive, even though she had not been barren.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There was nothing at all from which any hope could be drawn.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But he looked to Him That gave the promise, and believed what he did not see.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, what he believed, we see.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore from these things which we see, we ought to believe what we see not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He begat Isaac, we saw it not; and Isaac begat Jacob, and this we did not see; and Jacob begat twelve sons, and them we saw not; and his twelve sons begat the people of Israel; this great people we see.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I have now begun to mention those things which we do see.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Of the people of Israel was born the Virgin Mary, and she gave birth to Christ; and, lo, in Christ all nations are blessed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What more true? more certain? more plain?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Together with me, long after the world to come, ye who have been gathered together out of the nations.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In this world hath God fulfilled His promise concerning the seed of Abraham.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How shall He not give us His eternal promises, whom He hath made to be Abraham’s seed?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For this the Apostle saith: “But if ye be Christ’s” (they are the Apostle’s words), “then are ye Abraham’s seed.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We have begun to be some great thing; let no man despise himself: we were once nothing; but we are something.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We have said unto the Lord, “Remember that we are dust;” but out of the dust He made man, and to dust He gave life, and in Christ our Lord hath He already brought this same dust to the Kingdom of Heaven.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For from this dust took He flesh, from this took earth, and hath raised earth to heaven, He who made heaven and earth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If then these two new things, not yet done, were set before us, and it were asked of us, “Which is the most wonderful, that He who is God should be made Man, or he who is man should be made a man of God? which is the more wonderful? which the more difficult?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What hath Christ promised us?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That which as yet we see not; that is, that we should be His men, and reign with Him, and never die?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is so to say with difficulty believed, that a man once born should arrive at that life, where he shall never die.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is what we believe with a heart well cleansed, cleansed, I mean, of the world’s dust; that this dust close not up our eye of faith.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This it is that we are bid believe, that after we have been dead, we shall be even with our dead bodies in life, where we shall never die.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wonderful it is; but more wonderful is that which Christ hath done.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For which is the more incredible, that man should live for ever, or that God should ever die?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That men should receive life from God is the more credible; that God should receive death from men I suppose is the more incredible.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet this hath been brought to pass already: let us then believe that which is to be.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If that which is the more incredible hath been brought to pass, shall He not give us that which is the more credible?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For God hath power to make of men Angels, who hath made of earthy and filthy spawn, men.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What shall we be?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What have we been?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I am ashamed to call it to mind; I am forced to consider it, yet I blush to tell it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What have we been?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whence did God make men?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What were we before we were at all?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We were nothing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When we were in our mother’s wombs, what were we?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is enough that ye remember.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Withdraw your minds from the whence ye were made, and think of what ye are.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye live; but so do herbs and trees live.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye have sensation, and so have cattle sensation.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye are men, ye have got beyond the cattle, ye are superior to the cattle; for that ye understand how great things He hath done for you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye have life, ye have sensation, ye have understanding, ye are men.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now to this benefit what can be compared?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye are Christians.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if we had not received this, what would it profit us, that we were men!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then we are Christians, we belong to Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For all the world’s rage, it doth not break us; because we belong to Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For all the world’s caresses, it doth not seduce us; we belong to Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A great Patron have we found, Brethren.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye know that men depend much upon their patrons.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A dependent of a man in power will make answer to any one who threatens him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Thou canst do nothing to me, as long as my lord’s head is safe.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How much more boldly and surely may we say, “Thou canst do nothing to us, whilst our Head is safe.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Forasmuch as our Patron is our Head.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whosoever depend upon any man as patron, are his dependents; we are the members of our Patron.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let Him bear us in Himself, and let no man tear us away from Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Since what labours soever we shall have endured in this world, all that passeth away, is nothing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The good things shall come which shall not pass away; by labours we arrive at them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But when we have arrived, no one teareth us away from them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The gates of Jerusalem are shut; they receive the bolts too, that to that city it may be said, “Praise the Lord, O Jerusalem, praise thy God, O Sion.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He hath strengthened the bolts of thy gates; He hath blessed thy children within thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who hath made thy borders peace.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When the gates are shut, and the bolts drawn, no friend goeth out, no enemy entereth in.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There shall we have true and assured security, if here we shall not have abandoned the truth.
